active_topics.name: mimo-push-gp-v2-structured-bp-fixed
active_topics.status: cpp_test4 COMPLETED (40 gens). Best genome: gen-4, BER=0.05453, ratio=0.459. FULL ANALYSIS DONE: Evolved genome = Clamped A* (score=max(cum_dist,0.348)). formula analysis corrected (program_to_formula had multi-stack bug). Ablations (200 trials) confirm: F_up dead weight, F_down=cumulative dist critical, floor EC1=0.348 helps ~10% over pure A*. cpp_test5 ready to launch (seed from gen-4, max_nodes=1200, 60 gens).
active_topics.next_action: 1. Launch cpp_test5 (run_test5.bat, seed from gen-4, 60 gens, max_nodes=1200) to find more sophisticated scoring beyond clamped A*. 2. Evolve toward R-matrix-aware scoring (column norms, interference cancellation). 3. Consider disabling H_halt or forcing max_bp_iters>=2 to prevent 1-iteration degenerate case. 4. After test5, analyze if new programs access R matrix or use multi-iteration BP.

global_notes: Structured BP v2 architecture: 4-program genome (F_down, F_up, F_belief, H_halt). Full-tree BP sweeps: UP (leaves-root), DOWN (root-leaves), score frontier. C++ DLL evaluator (evaluator_bp.dll) for fast evolution. Key insight: current best genome is CLAMPED A* — genuinely novel BP message passing not yet achieved. The system works correctly. program_to_formula fixed to track multi-stack (float/int/bool/node). Next challenge: evolve programs that use R matrix context or multi-pass BP.
